recommendation_rationale: BT-03 (PT private oncology white-label, CUF/Lusiadas/Luz Saude) is the highest-composite, fastest-to-revenue option whose required pilot most closely matches the existing 8-12w PoV in BCS senology follow-up: it consumes problem.md v2's causal hypothesis (triagem episodica -> sintomas escalam) directly, the buyer (head of senology / oncology operations) has a budgeted line, and EV-047 already pre-validates the price point (>=2 hospitals @ >=EUR 200/mo). It also generates the clinical evidence and proprietary BCS data asset that unlock BT-01 (DiGA) and BT-04 (pharma) later, satisfying the Trojan-Horse-with-expansion test.
wedge_summary: BCS in months 0-24 post active treatment, in PT hospital senology/oncology follow-up, between scheduled consultations - the exact friction window where the causal hypothesis 'triagem episodica -> sintomas escalam sem detecao' bites, and where PerSense's N=126 / AUC 0.78 moat applies.

theses.name: DiGA companion-DTx for BCS survivorship (DE -> EU)
theses.one_line: BfArM-listed adaptive DTx that detects between-consultation affective relapse in breast-cancer survivors, reimbursed per prescription by German GKV.
theses.buyer_user_split: GKV pays; oncologist/senologist or GP prescribes; BCS patient uses; hospital senology service refers
theses.revenue_model: Per-prescription, 90-day prescription cycle, reimbursed annually per active patient

price_point_hypothesis.note: lower-mid band of EV-060 EUR 200-600/patient/year DiGA reimbursement; conservative for an oncology-survivorship indication where 42% of approved DiGA are mental-health [EV-035] and Zanadio precedent exists [EV-032]

tam_serviceable.calc: DE BCS prevalent survivors first 24mo ~125-150K (scaled from EU 750K [EV-020] x DE pop share ~17%) x 30-40% with clinical anxiety/depression [EV-019] = 37.5K-60K addressable. Assume 20-35% prescription penetration over 3 yr (anchored on Limbic 33% NHS adoption [EV-026]) = 7.5K-21K active patients/yr. At EUR 350/pt/yr [EV-060] => EUR 2.6-7.4M Yr3 DE-only run-rate; full DE ceiling EUR 13-21M; including EU expansion to 750K BCS [EV-020] same conversion => EUR 26-79M/yr serviceable

theses.defensibility: BCS-specific labelled training data (PerSense N=126 AUC 0.78 [EV-043]) + DiGA listing acts as a regulatory moat; 42% of approved DiGA are mental-health [EV-035] but none are BCS-specific; HelloBetter shows 30+ RCT evidence floor is the German norm [EV-027]
theses.pov_pilot_fit: PoV must produce DiGA-grade endpoints (PHQ-9/GAD-7 change scores, between-consultation alert precision, consent >=50% lifting the 34% iNNOVSensing baseline [EV-041]). The current 8-12w PoV in PT senology generates these signals, but DiGA evidence threshold ultimately needs an RCT - PoV is a *necessary precursor* not the dossier itself.
theses.wedge: BCS in DE oncology-aftercare (Nachsorge) clinics, months 0-24 post active treatment, prescribed at the 2nd post-treatment senology visit when patient is most at risk of unobserved affective relapse [problem.md v2 causal hypothesis].
theses.expansion_path: 1) Once DE-listed, fast-follow EU mutual recognition into PT (private oncology), ES (CAGR 18.5% [EV-076]), FR (EUR 0.7B mkt [EV-055]). 2) Extend label to colorectal and other solid-tumour survivorship using same JITAI mechanism, multiplying TAM 3-5x without retraining the regulatory pathway.
theses.buyer_pain: GKV: untreated post-cancer depression escalates to long-term sick leave / disability claims. Oncology = EUR 810M most-funded segment of EU digital health 2024 [EV-050] precisely because payers see survivorship cost spiral. <20% of BCS receive formal psych support today [EV-016] = ~30K untreated cases/yr in DE alone, each plausibly costing GKV 4-5 figures in unscheduled care.
top_risks: Pear/Mindstrong precedent: scaling before evidence destroyed $160M [EV-069] / $1.6B [EV-068]; DiGA may not survive an underpowered RCT.
MDR IIa cost EUR 300-700K + 24-36mo [EV-057, EV-058] requires bridging financing; Kintsugi burned $16M / 4yr in FDA [EV-070].
DE oncology-aftercare prescriber adoption is unproven for an algorithmic JITAI; HelloBetter precedent is for self-guided CBT, not adaptive sensing.
open_issues: AUC 0.78 [EV-043, internal_only] generalisation to a DE cohort unknown.
DiGA evidence requirements for an adaptive/ML product (vs static CBT) are evolving - acceptance of a learning algorithm not guaranteed.
No DE clinical partner currently identified.

theses.name: Pharma companion / BCS adherence research-services
theses.one_line: Sell PerSense as a deployed sensing+EMA platform to oncology pharma (Roche/Pfizer/Novartis/AstraZeneca) for adjuvant-therapy adherence and PRO-collection in BCS post-treatment cohorts.
theses.customer: Oncology pharma medical-affairs / RWE / clinical-development teams (Roche, Pfizer, Novartis, AstraZeneca)
theses.buyer_user_split: Pharma sponsor pays research-services contract; hospital is study site; BCS patient consents as study participant
theses.revenue_model: Fee-per-contract (per-study), milestone-based; recurring once first contract lands

price_point_hypothesis.value: EUR 40K/contract initial, scaling to EUR 150-300K/multi-site study
price_point_hypothesis.note: EV-073 EUR 20-60K/contract entry research services; multi-site oncology studies command 3-5x

tam_serviceable.calc: Top 4 oncology pharma each run 5-10 BCS adjuvant or survivorship studies/yr EU-wide. Capture 1-3 contracts/sponsor at EUR 100-300K avg => EUR 0.4-3.6M/sponsor; portfolio of 5-15 contracts/yr realistic => EUR 6-12M serviceable. Anchored on EV-073 (EUR 20-60K) and EV-050 (EUR 810M oncology = most-funded segment 2024)

regulatory_path.path: MDR Class I as research tool / GCP-aligned data collection; no DTx claim required initially
regulatory_path.cost_eur: EUR 30-60K (mindLAMP/Beiwe deployment + GDPR compliance); GDPR liability EUR 2.3M precedent [EV-062]

theses.defensibility: PerSense BCS-specific cohort + AUC 0.78 [EV-043] differentiates vs generic ePRO/Beiwe deployments; 2-hospital iNNOVSensing channel in PT is the network moat; PT validation 50% cheaper than NHS [EV-080]
theses.pov_pilot_fit: PoV directly de-risks: shows the platform collects high-fidelity sensing+EMA in BCS at >=70% completeness [EV-047], which is exactly the SLA pharma buys. The 8-12w pilot becomes the reference deployment for pharma RFP responses.
theses.wedge: First contract: a single PT- or EU-based oncology pharma medical-affairs study on adjuvant endocrine-therapy adherence in BCS months 0-24, where PerSense provides the passive sensing + adherence-EMA layer the sponsor cannot easily build in-house.
theses.expansion_path: 1) Once 1 contract delivers, expand to multi-cohort surveillance (colorectal, prostate) within same sponsor. 2) Use accumulated cross-sponsor RWE to underwrite a pharma-funded RCT that doubles as the DiGA dossier (BT-01) - pharma de-risks BT-01.
theses.buyer_pain: Pharma medical-affairs pain: adjuvant endocrine-therapy adherence in BCS is ~50% at 5 yr; every 10pp of adherence loss costs sponsor measurable revenue + harms outcomes. PerSense gives them context-sensitive adherence data the EMR doesn't capture. PT validation 50% cheaper than NHS (EUR 80-150K vs EUR 300K) [EV-080].
top_risks: Pharma sales cycles are 6-12mo and procurement-heavy; no warm intro yet documented.
Commoditisation: Beiwe ($3.50-80/participant/mo [EV-037]) and mindLAMP (EUR 30-50/mo [EV-038]) are open-source substitutes; differentiation must rest on BCS labelled-data + service delivery.
GDPR liability EUR 2.3M precedent [EV-062] - any breach is existential.
open_issues: No named pharma counterparty yet (Roche/Lundbeck only mentioned generically in EV-073).
Whether AUC 0.78 [EV-043] is a sellable proof-point for adherence (not just depression detection).
Whether team has BD bandwidth for pharma sales cycles in parallel with PoV execution.

theses.name: PT private oncology white-label (CUF / Lusiadas / Luz Saude)
theses.one_line: Subscription dashboard + JITAI sensing layer for BCS senology follow-up, sold per-hospital to PT private oncology operators that already differentiate on patient experience.
theses.customer: PT private hospital groups: head of oncology / senology / quality (CUF Oncologia, Lusiadas, Luz Saude)
theses.buyer_user_split: Hospital pays SaaS; senology team uses dashboard; BCS patient uses app; oncologist remains accountable physician
theses.revenue_model: SaaS per-hospital, monthly subscription + light setup; optional per-active-case overage

price_point_hypothesis.note: Anchored on PerSense Phase-2 hospital subscription EUR 500-2K/mo [EV-084], white-label B2B benchmark EUR 15-50K/yr [EV-083], and PoV success threshold of >=2 buyers @ >=EUR 200/mo [EV-047]; private oncology pays the upper band because the buyer is a margin centre

tam_serviceable.calc: PT private oncology = ~3 large groups (CUF, Lusiadas, Luz) x avg 8 senology-active sites = ~24 hospital-units. Capture 30-50% over 3 yr = 7-12 active sites @ EUR 800-1500/mo = EUR 67K-216K/yr Yr2; full PT private TAM EUR 0.5-1M/yr including overages and module expansion. PT digital-mental-health market EUR 40-60M [EV-074] gives ceiling sanity.

regulatory_path.path: MDR Class I as wellness/triage decision-support (no diagnostic claim) initially; upgrade to Class IIa once revenue funds it
regulatory_path.cost_eur: Class I self-certification + GDPR <EUR 30K initially; Class IIa upgrade EUR 300-700K [EV-057]

theses.defensibility: PerSense N=126 / AUC 0.78 [EV-043] is unique in PT BCS; iNNOVSensing relationships at 2 PT hospitals are the channel head-start; PT cost-of-validation 50% lower than NHS [EV-080] makes evidence accumulation cheap
theses.pov_pilot_fit: Highest fit: the 8-12w PoV [EV-046] is *literally* a pilot deployment at PT senology hospital(s). The PoV success metric (>=2 hospitals/therapists pay >=EUR 200/mo [EV-047]) is the exact contract-conversion test for this thesis. No re-design required.
theses.wedge: One CUF or Luz senology unit, BCS in months 0-24 post-treatment, between scheduled consultations - the exact friction in problem.md v2 ('triagem episodica -> sintomas escalam'). Buyer signs because <20% of their BCS receive formal psych support [EV-016] and the gap is visible in their NPS / quality reporting.
theses.expansion_path: 1) Once 2-3 senology units live, layer pharma research-services contracts (BT-02) on the same deployment - same data, second buyer. 2) Once 6-12 mo of clinical evidence is accumulated cheaply [EV-080], it underwrites the BT-01 DiGA dossier in DE - hospital revenue funds regulatory.
theses.buyer_pain: Private oncology head: BCS retention/NPS is the differentiator vs SNS; an undetected affective relapse = churned patient + complaint. <20% of BCS get formal psych support today [EV-016]; head can't add a psycho-oncologist FTE (~EUR 60-80K/yr) but can pay EUR 10-18K/yr for a SaaS that catches the same signal earlier.
top_risks: PT private market is small (3 groups); concentration risk - losing one buyer halves the TAM.
Free-rider clinicians: dashboards adopted but not used; engagement collapses (Limbic-style integration into referral pathway [EV-026] required).
Regulatory drift: as evidence claims grow, MDR Class IIa becomes mandatory and capex returns.
open_issues: Buyer-side number for clinician burden: how many alerts/week senology can absorb without new hires (problem.md v2 open issue 3).
Whether CUF/Lusiadas/Luz procurement actually closes within 6 mo - no signed LOI yet.
Whether BCS month-0-24 follow-up is sufficiently standardised across the 3 groups to deploy a single product.

theses.name: IPO / public oncology + EU-funded research consortium
theses.one_line: Deploy PerSense in PT public oncology (IPO Lisboa/Porto/Coimbra) under EU/PT non-dilutive funding (COMPETE 2030, EIC Transition, PRR) as a research-anchored revenue line.
theses.customer: IPO institutions and university hospitals as research-grant co-applicants; ultimate funder = COMPETE 2030 / EIC / FCT
theses.buyer_user_split: Grant pays the consortium; PerSense is paid project-line subcontractor; IPO is study site; BCS patient is research participant
theses.revenue_model: Project-based grant subcontracts; recurring via stacked grant cycles

price_point_hypothesis.note: Anchored on PerSense FCT EUR 300K [EV-044], PRODROMUS EUR 300K FLAD [EV-045], COMPETE 2030 / PRR up to EUR 4M [EV-079], EIC Transition EUR 2.5M [EV-065]

tam_serviceable.calc: 1-2 active grants at any time x EUR 300K-500K/grant amortised over 2-3 yr => EUR 200-500K/yr. Stretch to EUR 1-1.5M/yr with EIC Transition [EV-065] or COMPETE 2030 [EV-066] win. COMPETE 2030 dotation EUR 3.9B [EV-066] gives ceiling sanity.

regulatory_path.path: Research-only (ethics committee approval); no MDR required while research-only

theses.defensibility: PerSense BCS dataset + 2-hospital iNNOVSensing track record + active FCT [EV-044] make the consortium credible; competitors without a PT clinical anchor cannot bid into IPO-led calls
theses.pov_pilot_fit: PoV outputs (consent rate, data completeness, AUC replication) directly populate the next grant proposal. But pilot does not by itself produce revenue - first grant pays 9-15mo later. PoV-alignment moderate.
theses.wedge: BCS at IPO-Lisboa senology, replication of AUC 0.78 [EV-043] in a 3rd hospital under a PerSense-led FCT/COMPETE 2030 follow-on grant - same causal hypothesis (triagem episodica) but now with public-system buyer narrative.
theses.expansion_path: 1) Grant data feeds DiGA (BT-01) and pharma (BT-02) evidence dossiers at marginal cost. 2) Public-system success unlocks SNS contracting once a public-DTx pathway exists in PT (currently absent - acknowledged risk).
theses.buyer_pain: IPO directors: consortium-grade research output and EU programme participation are KPIs in their own right; SNS waiting times for psychology >300 days in some regions [EV-013] but no budget for solutions. Grants relieve the budget constraint.
top_risks: EIC Accelerator success rate ~5.9% [EV-063] - statistically most grants fail.
Grant revenue is lumpy and time-bounded; not a true commercial business.
PT lacks a public DTx reimbursement pathway [EV-074]; expansion to recurring SNS revenue is structurally blocked today.
open_issues: Whether team has bandwidth to write 2-3 grant proposals in parallel with commercial PoV.
Whether IPO PIs are committed (no signed LoI documented).
Whether grant cashflow can sustain MDR investment without dilutive top-up.

theses.name: Insurer survivorship benefit (Medis Oncologia / Multicare / AdvanceCare)
theses.one_line: PT private health insurer adds PerSense as a covered benefit inside oncology / survivorship plans, paying per-active-life to reduce escalation cost in BCS post-treatment.
theses.customer: PT private health insurers: Medis (Ageas), Multicare (Fidelidade), AdvanceCare
theses.buyer_user_split: Insurer pays per covered life; insured BCS patient enrols via oncology benefit; treating oncologist signs off
theses.revenue_model: Per-member-per-month (PMPM) for enrolled BCS lives, optionally outcomes-linked

price_point_hypothesis.note: Anchored on EV-083 EUR 5-15/active user/mo and Swiss Re/Wysa precedent [EV-036] of insurer-grade pricing; 40% of new mental-health partnerships are insurer-led [EV-072]

tam_serviceable.calc: PT private health insurance covers ~2.5M lives. BCS prevalence in working-age covered population ~0.3-0.5% => 7.5K-12.5K BCS covered lives. Of those, ~30-40% have clinical anxiety/depression [EV-019] = 2.3K-5K addressable. Insurer enrols 30-50% = 700-2500 active. At EUR 10/mo => EUR 84K-300K/yr per insurer; sum across 3 insurers and outcomes uplift => EUR 0.4-0.9M/yr. EU expansion (Medis-equivalents, German PKV) multiplies 10-20x.

regulatory_path.path: MDR Class I (wellness) initially; GDPR critical given insurer data flow

theses.defensibility: Once one insurer signs an exclusive oncology-benefit, it locks the others out for 12-24 months; PerSense BCS-specific evidence [EV-043] differentiates vs Wysa/Limbic generic offerings
theses.pov_pilot_fit: PoV in hospital gives utilization + clinical-endpoint signal but does NOT produce the cost-of-care reduction figure insurers ultimately want. To convert this thesis would require an additional insurer-side data exercise post-PoV. Moderate alignment.
theses.wedge: Medis Oncologia (existing dedicated oncology product line) - smallest distance from current Medis offering to a covered DTx benefit; BCS post-treatment cohort is already segmented in their product structure.
theses.expansion_path: 1) From BCS extend to all-cancer survivorship in same insurer. 2) Cross-sell to corporate/group plans with employer pain (burnout EU EUR 600B/yr [EV-077]) - generalises PerSense beyond oncology.
theses.buyer_pain: Insurer actuarial pain: cost-of-care for untreated BCS depression escalates to ER, sick-leave, claims; Wysa/Swiss Re showed -30% depression rates [EV-036] - insurer-grade KPI. Health insurers are the most active partner type globally (40% of new mental-health partnerships in 2024 [EV-072]).
top_risks: PT insurer procurement is slow and politically sensitive; integration into claims systems is a 9-12mo IT effort.
No outcome data linking JITAI specifically to BCS cost-of-care reduction (extrapolation from Wysa generic [EV-031, EV-036]).
Insurer may demand exclusivity that blocks BT-03 hospital channel.
open_issues: Cost-of-care delta for untreated BCS depression in PT private market is not quantified.
Whether BCS-specific benefit is actuarially viable at the small PT covered-lives base.
